Level 1: Disclosed: Authors stated whether or not a study was registered—and, if so, where and when it was registered.
Level 2: Shared and Cited: Researchers registered the study and cited the registration.
Level 3: Certified: A party independent from the researchers certified that the study was registered at an appropriate time and the registration was complete per best-practice for the study design.

Level 1: Disclosed: Authors stated whether or not the study protocol is available—and, if so, where and when it was shared.
Level 2: Shared and Cited: Researchers publicly shared the study protocol and cited the protocol.
Level 3: Certified: A party independent from the researchers certified that the study protocol was shared at an appropriate time and the study protocol was complete per best-practice for the study design.

Level 1: Disclosed: Authors stated whether or not the analysis plan is available—and, if so, where and when it was shared.
Level 2: Shared and Cited: Researchers publicly shared the analysis plan and cited the analysis plan.
Level 3: Certified: A party independent from the researchers certified that the analysis plan was shared at an appropriate time and the analysis plan was complete per best-practice for the study design.

Level 1: Disclosed: Authors stated whether or not materials are available—and, if so, where.
Level 2: Shared and Cited: Researchers cited materials deposited in a trusted repository by themselves or others.
Level 3: Certified: A party independent from the researchers certified that materials were deposited and documented per best-practice for the type of materials.

Level 1: Disclosed: Authors stated whether or not data are available—and, if so, where.
Level 2: Shared and Cited: Researchers cited data deposited in a trusted repository by themselves or others.
Level 3: Certified: A party independent from the researchers certified that data were deposited with metadata per best-practice for the type of data.

Level 1: Disclosed: Authors stated whether or not analytic code is available—and, if so, where.
Level 2: Shared and Cited: Researchers cited analytic code deposited in a trusted repository by themselves or others.
Level 3: Certified: A party independent from the researchers certified that analytic code was deposited and documented per relevant best-practice.

Level 1: Disclosed: Authors stated whether or not they used a reporting guideline—and, if so, which guideline.
Level 2: Shared and Cited: Authors publicly shared a completed reporting guideline checklist and cited the reporting guideline.
Level 3: Certified: A party independent from the researchers certified that the researchers adhered to the appropriate reporting guideline for the study design.
